text.title: Project Documentation Convention Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the project-docs-convention methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every `CLAUDE.md` MUST contain exactly the line `@AGENTS.md` (or `@AGENTS.md` plus a one-line note); nothing else.
rule.rationale: Claude Code's @-ref pattern resolves at load time; keeping CLAUDE.md to one line makes the convention language-agnostic + future-proof.

rule.statement: `AGENTS.md` MUST be 20-80 lines. Past 80 lines, content moves to `.agents/<topic>.md` and AGENTS.md indexes them.
rule.rationale: Auto-loaded context burns prompt budget; the 20-80 cap keeps it skimmable + cheap.

rule.statement: Every directory containing source code MUST carry the CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md pair. Empty `__init__.py`-only dirs are the only exemption.
rule.rationale: Per-module coverage lets agents work effectively in subpackages without re-reading the whole repo root.

rule.statement: Every `AGENTS.md` MUST contain at least: one-line dir purpose, file table (what's here), key types / commands, gotchas (if any). Required headings are deterministic so validators can check.
rule.rationale: Skipping required sections defeats the convention; deterministic headings let CI gate the docs.

rule.statement: Detailed architecture / API references / decision records MUST live in `.agents/` with an `INDEX.md`. AGENTS.md links to them by relative path.
rule.rationale: Separating essential (AGENTS.md) from deep (`.agents/`) keeps the auto-load budget tight while preserving discoverability.
